name: out-of-africa-dispersal
description: Modern humans spread from Africa across Eurasia, the migration from which most non-Africans descend.

name: humans-reach-australia
description: Seafaring humans crossed open water to settle the continent of Sahul, founding Aboriginal Australia.

name: humans-settle-near-oceania
description: Early voyagers reached New Guinea and the Bismarck Archipelago, the first peopling of the Pacific.

name: humans-enter-europe
description: Modern humans spread into Europe alongside Neanderthals, founding the continent's earliest populations.

name: humans-settle-japan
description: Early peoples crossed into the Japanese archipelago, beginning the long human presence in Japan.

name: humans-reach-siberia
description: Humans adapted to the frozen Arctic, settling Siberia and reaching the gateway to the Americas.

name: peopling-beringia
description: Migrants crossed the Bering land bridge from Asia into the Americas, the first Americans.

name: settlement-south-america
description: Early Americans reached the southern continent, with sites like Monte Verde marking the spread.

name: settlement-caribbean-islands
description: Migrants from the mainland began settling the Caribbean islands by sea over many centuries.

name: neolithic-farmers-europe
description: Anatolian farmers spread agriculture across Europe, replacing many hunter-gatherer populations.

name: austronesian-expansion-begins
description: Seafarers from Taiwan began spreading across Southeast Asia and the Pacific, the great ocean migration.

name: yamnaya-migration-europe
description: Steppe herders spread west into Europe, reshaping its ancestry and spreading Indo-European languages.

name: settlement-of-madagascar
description: Austronesian voyagers from Borneo crossed the Indian Ocean to settle the island of Madagascar.

name: sea-peoples-migrations
description: Displaced seafaring groups moved across the eastern Mediterranean during the Bronze Age collapse.

name: indo-aryan-migration
description: Indo-European speakers moved into the Indian subcontinent, shaping its languages and culture.

name: greek-colonization-mediterranean
description: Greek city-states founded colonies around the Mediterranean and Black Sea, spreading their culture.

name: phoenician-colonization
description: Phoenician traders founded colonies across the Mediterranean, including Carthage in North Africa.

name: bantu-reach-southern-africa
description: The centuries-long Bantu migration reached the far south of Africa, spreading farming and ironworking.

name: germanic-migration-period
description: Germanic peoples moved into the Roman Empire, an era of upheaval that reshaped Europe's map.

name: huns-migrate-into-europe
description: Nomadic Huns swept west from Central Asia, displacing Germanic tribes toward the Roman frontier.

name: slavic-migrations
description: Slavic peoples spread across Eastern Europe and the Balkans, settling vast new territories.

name: settlement-of-polynesia-east
description: Voyagers reached the far islands of eastern Polynesia, including the Society and Cook Islands.

name: settlement-of-hawaii
description: Polynesian voyagers navigated thousands of miles of open ocean to settle the Hawaiian Islands.

name: magyars-settle-hungary
description: The nomadic Magyars migrated into the Carpathian Basin, founding the Hungarian homeland.

name: norse-settle-greenland
description: Erik the Red led settlers from Iceland to establish Norse colonies on the coast of Greenland.

name: maori-reach-new-zealand
description: Polynesian voyagers settled New Zealand, the last major landmass reached by humans, becoming the Maori.

name: moriori-settle-chathams
description: Polynesian settlers reached the remote Chatham Islands, becoming the distinct Moriori people.

name: expulsion-jews-from-spain
description: Spain expelled its Jews, scattering the Sephardi people across North Africa, the Levant, and Europe.

name: transatlantic-slave-trade-begins
description: Forced migration of enslaved Africans to the Americas began, ultimately uprooting over twelve million.

name: huguenot-diaspora
description: France revoked Protestant freedoms, driving Huguenots to flee to England, the Dutch Republic, and beyond.

name: puritan-great-migration
description: Thousands of Puritans sailed from England to New England, founding the Massachusetts Bay Colony.

name: expulsion-of-the-acadians
description: Britain deported French-speaking Acadians from Canada, many resettling in Louisiana as the Cajuns.

name: scotch-irish-migration
description: Ulster Scots emigrated to the American colonies, settling the Appalachian backcountry frontier.

name: convict-transport-australia
description: Britain began shipping convicts to Australia, seeding the first large European settlement there.

name: great-trek-south-africa
description: Dutch-descended Boers migrated inland from the British Cape Colony to found new republics.

name: irish-famine-emigration
description: The Great Famine drove over a million Irish to emigrate, transforming America's cities.

name: mormon-trek-to-utah
description: Persecuted Mormons trekked west across the plains to settle the Salt Lake Valley in Utah.

name: oregon-trail-migration
description: Settlers traveled the Oregon Trail by wagon train, opening the American West to mass migration.

name: freed-slaves-settle-liberia
description: Free African Americans resettled in West Africa, founding the colony that became Liberia.

name: european-emigration-peak
description: Millions of Europeans emigrated to the United States in a wave that peaked at the turn of the century.

name: chinese-emigration-gold-rush
description: Chinese laborers emigrated to California during the gold rush, building Chinatowns across the West.

name: indentured-labor-diaspora
description: Indian indentured laborers were shipped to colonies worldwide, settling the Caribbean, Fiji, and beyond.

name: great-migration-black-americans
description: Millions of Black Americans moved from the rural South to northern cities seeking work and freedom.

name: dust-bowl-migration
description: Drought drove hundreds of thousands of farmers to flee the Great Plains for California.

name: partition-of-india-migration
description: Partition triggered one of history's largest migrations, with millions crossing the India-Pakistan border.

name: windrush-arrival
description: The Empire Windrush brought Caribbean migrants to Britain, beginning postwar Commonwealth settlement.

name: palestinian-exodus
description: Around 700,000 Palestinians fled or were expelled during the war that founded Israel, the Nakba.

name: post-war-german-expulsions
description: Millions of ethnic Germans were expelled from Eastern Europe at the close of the Second World War.

name: bracero-program-begins
description: The US recruited millions of Mexican guest workers under the Bracero labor migration program.

name: gastarbeiter-migration
description: West Germany recruited Turkish and southern European guest workers to rebuild its postwar economy.

name: vietnamese-boat-people
description: Hundreds of thousands fled Vietnam by sea after the war, resettling across the world as refugees.

name: soviet-jewish-emigration
description: As the USSR collapsed, hundreds of thousands of Soviet Jews emigrated to Israel and the United States.

name: european-migrant-crisis
description: Over a million refugees, many fleeing Syria, crossed the Mediterranean and Balkans to reach Europe.

name: settlement-of-fiji
description: Lapita seafarers settled the islands of Fiji, a key staging point for the wider Pacific migration.

name: thule-reach-greenland
description: Thule Inuit migrated east across Arctic Canada to Greenland, ancestors of today's Inuit.

name: vandals-migrate-to-africa
description: The Vandal people migrated across Europe and the sea to settle and rule Roman North Africa.

name: anglo-saxons-settle-britain
description: Germanic Angles, Saxons, and Jutes migrated to Britain, founding the kingdoms of early England.

name: cuban-exile-to-miami
description: After Castro's revolution, waves of Cubans fled to Miami, transforming the city's culture.

name: gold-rush-to-australia
description: Gold discoveries drew a flood of migrants to Australia, tripling the colony's population in a decade.
